prs.title: Added Google OAuth, and authentication chnages
[ENH] Add missing unit tests for sktime.utils.stats and sktime.utils.unique_str
prs.body:
#### Reference Issues/PRs
Resolves #9685

#### What does this implement/fix? Explain your changes.
Adds unit tests for two utility modules that had no test coverage:

- [sktime/utils/tests/test_stats.py](cci:7://file:///Users/oashe/Desktop/sktime/sktime/utils/tests/test_stats.py:0:0-0:0) — 15 tests for the weighted stats functions (`_weighted_percentile`, `_weighted_geometric_mean`, `_weighted_median`, `_weighted_min`, `_weighted_max`). Covers correctness against numpy/scipy, edge cases like concentrated weights and constant arrays, shape validation, and ordering invariants.
- [sktime/utils/tests/test_unique_str.py](cci:7://file:///Users/oashe/Desktop/sktime/sktime/utils/tests/test_unique_str.py:0:0-0:0) — 6 tests for [_make_strings_unique](cci:1://file:///Users/oashe/Desktop/sktime/sktime/utils/unique_str.py:3:0-28:18). Covers basic dedup, chain collisions with the recursive counter, empty input, and substring non-conflict.

#### Does your contribution introduce a new dependency? If yes, which one?
No.

#### What should a reviewer concentrate their feedback on?
- Whether the test cases cover the right edge cases
- Whether the test structure follows sktime conventions properly

#### Did you add any tests for the change?
Yes — this PR is entirely about adding tests. 21 new tests total, all passing locally.

#### Any other comments?
Both modules had zero direct test coverage before this. Tests use `run_test_module_changed` guards and `pytest.mark.parametrize` where appropriate.

#### PR checklist

##### For all contributions
- [x] I've added myself to the [list of contributors](https://github.com/sktime/sktime/blob/main/CONTRIBUTORS.md) with any new badges I've earned :-)
- [x] The PR title starts with either [ENH], [MNT], [DOC], or [BUG]. [MNT] - CI, test framework.

prs.title: added units for resoucescontrollers
prs.body:
## Description

Add unit tests for [esp/esp/program/controllers/resources.py](cci:7://file:///Users/oashe/Desktop/devsite/esp/esp/program/controllers/resources.py:0:0-0:0), which had 0% test coverage. The [ResourceController](cci:2://file:///Users/oashe/Desktop/devsite/esp/esp/program/controllers/resources.py:42:0-110:22) class manages core program infrastructure — timeslots, resource types, classrooms, and floating equipment.

Tests are organized into 6 test classes:
- **TimeslotTest**: create, edit, and delete timeslots; bad ID handling
- **ResTypeTest**: create, edit, and delete resource types; choice passthrough
- **DeleteClassroomTest**: cascading cleanup (sections unscheduled, meeting times cleared, rooms deleted across timeslots)
- **AddClassroomTest**: form delegation with and without furnishings
- **DeleteEquipmentTest**: deletion across all program time blocks
- **ResourceControllerInitTest**: program reference storage

## Related Issue

Closes #4842

## Type of Change

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] New feature
- [ ] Breaking change
- [ ] Documentation update
- [ ] Refactor / cleanup
- [ ] CI/CD or build change

## Testing

- [x] Existing tests pass
- [x] New tests added (if applicable)
- [x] Manually verified

## AI Disclosure

GitHub Copilot: test structure suggested by AI
verified manually with full test passing

## Checklist

- [x] Self-reviewed the code
- [x] Updated documentation (if needed)
- [x] No new warnings or errors introduced

prs.title: fix: preview multiple instances in sampler
prs.body:
## Description
Fix audio resource issue in Sampler widget's AI Sample Generation feature.

## Problem
The Preview button in the AI Sample Generation dialog creates a new `Audio` object on every click without stopping or cleaning up the previous one. This causes:
- Multiple audio instances playing simultaneously (overlapping sounds)
- Unnecessary memory usage during playback
- Poor user experience

## Solution
- Added `audioPreview` variable to track the current audio instance
- Stop and reset any previously playing audio before starting new playback
- Clean up the reference when audio playback ends 

## Changes
- [js/widgets/sampler.js](cci:7://file:///Users/oashe/Desktop/musicblocks/js/widgets/sampler.js:0:0-0:0)

## Testing
- Verified in browser that clicking Preview multiple times no longer causes overlapping audio.

prs.title: chore: removed debug console log and replaced deprecated substr
prs.body:
## Description

This PR improves code quality in the AI Debugger widget ([js/widgets/aidebugger.js](cci:7://file:///Users/oashe/Desktop/musicblocks/js/widgets/aidebugger.js:0:0-0:0)).

### Changes Made

1. **Removed debug console.log statement** (Line 43)
   - The statement `console.log("AI Debugger Backend URL:", BACKEND_CONFIG.BASE_URL)` was exposing backend configuration information in the browser console
   - This is a security best practice for production code

2. **Replaced deprecated `String.prototype.substr()`** (Line 97)
   - Changed `substr(2, 9)` to `substring(2, 11)` in [_generateConversationId()](cci:1://file:///Users/oashe/Desktop/musicblocks/js/widgets/aidebugger.js:90:4-97:6) function
   - `substr()` is deprecated according to [MDN Web Docs](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/String/substr)

### Testing
- [x] I have read and followed the project's [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/sugarlabs/sugar-docs/blob/master/src/contributing.md).
- [x] `npm run lint` - Passed
- [x] `npm test` - All tests passed

### Files Modified

- [js/widgets/aidebugger.js]

Permission for using the logo was explicitly granted in the issue by the Dash0 co-founder.

prs.title: feat: Copy/Clickboard button in code snippets
prs.body:
Add Copy Button for JavaScript Code Snippets

This pull request adds a top-right “Copy” button to all JavaScript code snippets in the documentation.

✔ Features Implemented

Adds a floating copy button inside each <pre><code> block containing JavaScript.

Button appears only when JavaScript is enabled (JS progressively enhances the page).

Button is positioned consistently at the top-right corner, regardless of code length or wrapping.

Follows the implementation pattern already used in this repo.

issues.title: [ENH] Add missing unit tests for `sktime.utils.stats` and `sktime.utils.unique_str`
issues.body:
## Problem
`sktime.utils.stats` and `sktime.utils.unique_str` don't have any direct unit tests right now.

Functions like `_weighted_percentile`, `_weighted_geometric_mean`, `_weighted_median`, `_weighted_min`, `_weighted_max` in [stats.py](cci:7://file:///Users/oashe/Desktop/sktime/sktime/utils/tests/test_stats.py:0:0-0:0), and [_make_strings_unique](cci:1://file:///Users/oashe/Desktop/sktime/sktime/utils/unique_str.py:3:0-28:18) in [unique_str.py](cci:7://file:///Users/oashe/Desktop/sktime/sktime/utils/unique_str.py:0:0-0:0) are used internally across the codebase but nothing actually tests them on their own.

## Proposed Solution
Add two test files:

**[test_stats.py](cci:7://file:///Users/oashe/Desktop/sktime/sktime/utils/tests/test_stats.py:0:0-0:0)** — 15 tests:
- `_weighted_percentile`: uniform weights vs `np.median`, 2D column independence, concentrated weight, boundary percentiles (0, 100)
- `_weighted_geometric_mean`: checked against `scipy.stats.gmean`, single element identity, heavy weight dominance, shape mismatch errors
- `_weighted_median` / `_weighted_min` / `_weighted_max`: min ≤ median ≤ max ordering, constant data identity, data range bracketing

**[test_unique_str.py](cci:7://file:///Users/oashe/Desktop/sktime/sktime/utils/tests/test_unique_str.py:0:0-0:0)** — 6 tests:
- No-conflict passthrough, single collision, chain collision with counter logic, empty list, substring non-conflict

## Impact
Better test coverage for these core utility modules. Minimal CI risk since these are pure utility functions with no side effects.

issues.title: [Feature]: Add unit tests for esp/program/controllers/resources.py
issues.body:
### Problem Statement

There is currenlty no test coverage for  `esp/esp/program/controllers/resources.py`.This file contains the `ResourceController` class, which manages core program infrastructure ie. timeslots, resource types, classrooms. These are impoortant operation that affect scheduling and program setup.

### Proposed Solution

Add unit tests for the `ResourceController` class in `esp/esp/program/controllers/resources.py`.

The tests will cover:

- Timeslot creation, editing, and deletion
- Resource type CRUD and ProtectedError handling
- Classroom deletion/verifying with cascading section unschedule
- Classroom creation with furnishings
- Equipment deletion across program time blocks


### Alternatives Considered

Leaving the file without test coverage could allow regressions in classroom/timeslot management to go unnoticed, potentially breaking program scheduling.

### Additional Context

_No response_

issues.title: Add "Copy to Clipboard" button for code blocks in helmet.js documentation
issues.body:
Feature Request: Add "Copy to Clipboard" Button for Code Blocks in helmet.js Documentation
Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.
When following the [helmet.js documentation](https://helmetjs.github.io/), there are several code examples and setup commands for example, in the installation and starter code sections but no “Copy” button is provided for the code blocks.

This makes it slightly inconvenient for users who want to quickly copy shell commands or example code when setting up their projects.


Visual inspiration
Similar behavior can be seen in the documentation sites for:

- [React](https://react.dev/)
- [Vite](https://vitejs.dev/)
- [TailwindCSS](https://tailwindcss.com/)

Having a small clipboard icon button next to each of these code blocks would allow users to quickly copy the full command without manually selecting text.

Additional context
This enhancement will make the [helmet.js documentation](https://helmetjs.github.io/) more polished, modern, and beginner-friendly.

I’d love to work on this feature and contribute a PR to add copy buttons across all code blocks!
